tweets.text:
The most expensive thing in ecom:

It isn't bad ads or questionable hires

It's the 3 months u spent "getting ready" instead of shipping.

The brands doing $5M+/month all have one thing in common:

They stopped asking "what do our customers want" and started telling them.

The "perfect" product launch doesn't exist.

I know founders sitting on products for 6 months tweaking the logo, the copy, the color palette.

Meanwhile some 19yo shipped a simple Shopify store in a weekend and is doing $80K/month.

Ship it now. Fix it when ur rich.
